body.title: [sprint] Pure billing-type policy router (replaces rules_engine OR tiers)
body.intention: I intend that a pure, unit-provable router picks a provider+model from billing-type policy and the install's available providers — and that finishing it brings Igor back, proven.
body.description:
**Problem:** rules_engine.py hardcodes every tier to openrouter-primary; device.py's unknown-model fallthrough also targets openrouter. With OR off, the proxy fails closed in front of working sources (ollama_cloud, google_free). This is THE bug behind dark audits, ticket-file IntentExtractor errors, and Igor's silence.

**Shape:** Pure function `route(request, available_providers, policy) -> (provider, model)`. Policy = preference order over billing TYPES (default local -> subscription -> usage, cheapest-usage-first), constrained to providers meeting the request's capability tier. Replace rules_engine's hardcoded OR/anthropic tiers; fix device.py fallthrough to use the router (not openrouter). Deterministic -> unit-provable. Gated behind the provider/billing model + legacy-ID map (so real traffic resolves).

**Affected files:** devices/inference/rules_engine.py (replace OR tiers), devices/inference/device.py (dispatch fallthrough), policy default.
**Design rules:** pure/deterministic; policy over billing types (general); no provider hardcoded.
**Scope boundary:** IN — pure router + default policy + replace rules_engine OR tiers + fix fallthrough. OUT — the cost-gate budget check internals (T-inference-usage-cost-gate; router calls into it); reliability layer.
**Test plan:** router UNIT PROOFS on CONFLICT cases (not happy-path): (a) usage provider over-budget -> skipped; (b) preferred provider down -> falls to next billing tier; (c) capability need excludes a too-weak local model; (d) a request carrying a legacy concrete model ID -> routes to an available provider, not an error. LIVE: a real dispatch returns text; an Igor chat round-trip yields a response.
**Completion criteria:** rules_engine has NO hardcoded openrouter/anthropic primary tiers; router is a pure fn with unit proofs covering conflict cases (a)-(d); a live dispatch through the proxy returns text; an Igor round-trip returns a response (Igor talks again — proven).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**[reconcile 2026-06-23]** SUPERSEDED: rules_engine.route() already routes by tier, skips unavailable sources, prefers flat_rate, has a foreground flag — verified working (route('analyst')->ollama_cloud). Routing gaps absorbed into T-inference-resolve-requests-by-tier.
